invariants.statement: Queue order is deterministic, replayable, and honest under overload.
invariants.residualRisk: No unresolved repository-owned queue-governance defect remained after 272 rerun.

invariants.statement: Duplicate clustering and duplicate authority stay bound to canonical evidence.
invariants.residualRisk: Duplicate review remains bounded to DuplicateReviewSnapshot and append-only resolution decisions.

invariants.statement: Only one current owner may mutate a live task while others remain read-only.
invariants.residualRisk: Mutation remains fenced to the live lease and read-only recovery is browser-proven in 276.

invariants.statement: More-info, re-safety, endpoint decision, approval, escalation, callback, messaging, self-care, admin resolution, reopen, and support replay stay bound to their canonical objects.
invariants.residualRisk: Live adapter onboarding remains outstanding, but the canonical object boundaries themselves are enforced and proven in repository execution.

invariants.statement: Route publication, trust drift, continuity evidence, focus protection, and task completion settlement all fail closed in the same shell.
invariants.residualRisk: Seed-backed read helpers remain, but same-shell failure and recovery semantics are enforced and browser-proven.

invariants.statement: PHI-safe UI observability and redaction are proven for critical transitions.
invariants.residualRisk: No evidence pack or browser artifact is allowed to publish patient names on hardening-safe routes.

invariants.statement: Accessibility, semantic coverage, performance, and multi-user read-only behaviour are proven across the dense workspace.
invariants.residualRisk: The 276 hardening suite fixed all repository-owned defects it surfaced and finished within the declared budgets.
